final_response: Postmortem drafted at projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md; three immediate followups logged; decision proposed; observation captured.
actions: Step 1 — created tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/timeline.md with Detection / Diagnosis / Mitigation / Resolution headers

Step 2 — filled timeline.md with concrete 14:25–15:18 timestamps for the api@v3.42.0 staging deploy rollback (synthetic probe detection, flagclient@2.x diagnosis, rollback to api@v3.41.4, production deploy cancellation)
Step 3 — wrote factors.md with Contributing causes (4), Contributing context, and Things that worked sections, sourced from timeline.md
Step 4 — wrote actions.md with three immediate / three next-quarter / two aspiration action items, sourced from factors.md
Step 5 — composed final postmortem at projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md with cross-references to each intermediate artifact
Promoted three immediate action items to followups.md (pin flagclient to 1.x; tune poll interval; add fallback in resolveCheckoutVariant)
Logged a proposed decision in decisions.md (flagclient 2.x pin and fallback requirement)
Logged an observation in observations.md (staging as canary is load-bearing — second instance in six months)
Opened daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent, Log, and Handoff sections
notes: Followed the runbook step-by-step with the artifact-binding discipline: each step produced the named file before the next step read it.

Invented plausible incident details (release names, pod count, timestamps, library version) per task instructions to not pause asking the user — kept them internally consistent across the five intermediate and final artifacts.
Cross-references in the final postmortem use relative paths back into tmp/ even though tmp/ is gitignored — the in-session inspection point is what matters; the postmortem itself is durable.
Decision was logged as 'proposed' rather than 'accepted' because the user is the on-call running a postmortem solo; team sign-off has not happened yet. Used canonical status string 'proposed'.
Observation framing is descriptive (second instance of staging-as-canary catching a regression), not prescriptive — kept the bar between observation and decision intact.
Did not delete the 0000-00-00-example.md daybook seed; not in scope of this runbook.
Workspace conventions respected: ISO date filenames, kebab-case slug, canonical status strings, no invented enum variants.
